exemplar.title: How successful is the launching of Circular hub by Gucci help in promoting less wasteful fashion and increase its profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title: How successful is the launching of Circular hub by Gucci help in promoting less wasteful fashion and increase its profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Explicitly defines and consistently applies sustainability as the key concept
Clear analytical links between the concept and Gucciâs Circular Hub in multiple tools
Well-focused introduction and explicit concept identification on the title page
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally lapses into description without tying back to sustainability
Some discussion of the wider fashion industry is generic rather than concept-driven
Integration of the concept is strong but not sustained in every paragraph
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a strong understanding of sustainability and integrates it into analyses of Gucciâs Circular Hub in tools like the Ansoff Matrix, TBL and financial ratios. Occasional descriptive digressions and generic industry comments prevent full, seamless integration, placing this criterion firmly at Band 4 (4/5).

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents are provided, matching the optimum range
Documents are clearly relevant to the research question and are cited in the text
Includes a mix of financial reports, academic and corporate sources for triangulation
weaknesses: Depth varies across sources, with some documents (e.g., press releases) offering limited analytical richness
References are scattered in footnotes rather than a consolidated table
Citation details (e.g., market statistics) require more precise sourcing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA meets the rubricâs optimal quantity and relevance requirements, and most sources contribute meaningful depth. Although range of ideas could be broader and presentation of documents streamlined, the selection and use of five in-depth documents justify the top band for quantity and depth (4/4).

strengths: Effective and purposeful selection of three complementary tools (Ansoff Matrix, TBL, ratio analysis)
Each tool clearly relates to both sustainability and profitability aspects of the research question
Financial analysis table is well structured and facilitates pre-/post-launch comparison
weaknesses: Application of some tools (e.g., deeper TBL social aspects) is uneven
Benchmarking and quantitative justification (e.g., ratios) are limited
Some assertions (green-washing, tax avoidance) are not fully tied back to the selected tools
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Tool selection is strategic and aligns directly to the IAâs dual focus, satisfying Band 4. Application is generally effective, but occasional gaps in completeness (social dimension of TBL, benchmarking) mean the work straddles Band 3 and 4. On balance, the overall effectiveness merits 4/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Adequate extraction and use of multi-year financial data with qualitative support
Good initial links drawn between circular practices and cost reduction
Integration of qualitative reports adds dimension to numerical analysis
weaknesses: Relies on a single main data set without competitor or industry benchmarks
Key claims (e.g., microplastics release) are unsupported by citation
Limited synthesis across multiple tools undermines deeper evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis moves beyond description to interpret data and integrate qualitative evidence, matching Band 3. However, the lack of benchmarking, limited data variety and unsupported assertions prevent a higher mark.

strengths: Conclusion revisits each tool and explicitly answers the research question
Clear statement that the Circular Hub promotes sustainability but struggles with profitability
weaknesses: Some conclusions (e.g., green-washing, tax avoidance) are not fully backed by IA evidence
Linkage between evidence and every concluding claim could be stronger
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion provides an explicit answer to the research question (Band 3), but inconsistencies in supporting evidence limit it to Band 2 overall (2/3).

strengths: Comprehensive table of contents and numbered headings facilitate navigation
Logical progression of sections (Introduction â Tools â Conclusion)
weaknesses: Subheadings within tool analyses are sparse, impacting readability
Page numbering in the ToC requires final verification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA exhibits an appropriate and clear structure throughout, fully satisfying Band 2 for this criterion (2/2).

strengths: All required presentation elements are present (title page, word count, citations, bibliography)
Consistent inclusion of section numbering and references
weaknesses: Occasional colloquial language detracts from academic tone
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission includes every required element and is well formatted overall, earning full marks for Presentation despite minor style inconsistencies (2/2).

strandSelections.reasoning: A clear table of contents, numbered headings and logical progression (Intro â Tools â Conclusion) demonstrate an **appropriate overall structure**, earning the top mark for this strand.

The chosen documents (income statement, sustainability report, academic article, corporate release, magazine piece) are **clearly relevant** to the research question and are cited in the text.  Depth varies: two are internal press releases and the academic source is used mainly for green-washing claims, so the range of ideas is moderate rather than wide.  This places the work in the ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ category of Band 3.
The IA includes title page with word count, table of contents, numbered sections, bibliography, and citations, so **all required elements are present**, justifying the maximum score for presentation.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed, satisfying the rubricâs optimum range (3-5).  Therefore the highest mark band for quantity is appropriate.

Application shows clear relevanceâAnsoff is applied to market development, TBL to circular initiatives, ratios to pre- and post-launch figuresâyet analysis is uneven.  Some assertions (e.g., green-washing, tax avoidance) are not fully linked back to the Hubâs success, and numerical discussion lacks benchmarking.  Hence application is **adequate but not consistently effective**, consistent with Band 3.

The student extracts multi-year revenue, COGS and profit figures and integrates qualitative evidence from reports, giving an **adequate selection and use of data**.  However, only one data set is used and reliability of estimated margins is not discussed, preventing a higher band.

Analysis goes beyond description: the student links falling COGS to circular practices and evaluates consumer perceptions under Ansoff.  Still, arguments sometimes rely on assertion, and synthesis across tools is limited.  This reflects **âsome analysisâ with partial integration**, matching Band 3.
The final paragraph **explicitly answers the research question**, stating the Hubâs success in sustainability but limited profitability.  Therefore the highest band (explicit answer) is warranted.

The student selects three complementary toolsâAnsoff Matrix, Triple Bottom Line and ratio analysisâcovering strategic, sustainability and financial angles.  These are all well-established IB tools directly suited to the dual focus on sustainability and profitability, representing an **effective and purposeful selection** (Band 4).

Conclusions summarise each tool and claim the Hub promotes sustainability but is less profitable, which is **generally consistent** with the evidence presented.  Yet references to green-washing and tax avoidance are not backed by primary data in the IA, so consistency is partial, fitting Band 2.

Gucci and the Circular Hub are referenced consistently across every tool; data, examples and criticisms (tax, animal welfare) all stem from the organisation.  The commentary moves beyond description by analysing how the Hub affects cost of goods sold, target markets and supplier relations, demonstrating **strong, partially integrated analysis**.  Full integration is hindered by occasional generic discussion of the wider fashion industry, so Band 4 is most fitting.

The student explicitly chooses sustainability as the key concept, defines it and repeatedly links it to Gucciâs Circular Hub in the Ansoff, TBL and financial sections.  Sustainability is analysed in relation to recycled materials, circular production and cost control, showing a **strong conceptual grasp**.  However, integration is intermittentâsome paragraphs lapse into description (e.g., lengthy quotations on green-washing) without tying back to the conceptâso the analysis is not fully woven âthroughoutâ.  Hence a solid but not perfect performance aligns with Band 4.

annotations.comment: Strength: The key concept âSustainabilityâ is clearly identified on the title page, providing a focused lens for the analysis.
Suggestion: Refine the research question wording for clarity and grammatical accuracy (e.g., âDoes the launch of the Circular Hub help promote sustainable fashion and increase profitability?â).
Suggestion: Add a concise academic definition of sustainability immediately after the concept label to ground the analysis in theory.
Strength: The table of contents is comprehensive and logically organized, facilitating easy navigation through the IA.
Suggestion: Verify that all page numbers in the contents match the final pagination to avoid navigation errors.
Strength: The introduction explicitly defines the research context and mentions market growth, linking to Gucciâs Circular Hub launch.
Suggestion: Reorganize the supporting-documents list into a clear table in the main text rather than scattered footnotes for better readability.
Suggestion: Provide a precise citation for the market-worth statistic ("now worth over $6.5 Billion") to enhance reliability of quantitative claims.
Strength: Five supporting documents are listed in the table, meeting the optimum range for quantity and relevance.
Suggestion: Ensure each supporting document is uniquely numbered and avoid repeated references (e.g., duplicate âDocument 4â) to prevent confusion.
Suggestion: Complete the Ansoff matrix table with descriptive labels and concrete examples in each quadrant to improve clarity.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Suggestion: Explicitly link each quadrant discussion back to the sustainability concept to deepen conceptual integration.
Suggestion: Include a brief conclusion at the end of each tool section to summarize findings before moving on.
Strength: The financial analysis table clearly presents pre- and post-launch ratios, enabling straightforward comparison.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Suggestion: Compare Gucciâs profit margins to industry benchmarks to contextualize the impact of the Circular Hub.
Suggestion: Substantiate the claim that creative-director changes affected performance with specific evidence or internal sources.
Suggestion: Explicitly state how financial findings answer the research question to strengthen the conclusionâs linkage to evidence.
Strength: The conclusion section is structured to revisit each tool and clearly links findings back to the research question.
Suggestion: Include competitor or industry benchmarks when discussing the cost and usage of Econyl to contextualize Gucciâs performance.
Suggestion: Support claims about consumer attitudes toward circular fashion with empirical survey data or market research citations.
Suggestion: Justify the exclusion of the social aspect in the TBL more fully or include it to provide a complete Triple Bottom Line analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Weakness: The assertion that Econyl releases microplastics is made without supporting citation or dataâadd a source or remove the claim.
Suggestion: Present supplier and wage data in a concise table or chart to strengthen the economic dimension of the TBL.
Suggestion: Synthesize TBL findings with Ansoff and financial analysis in a summary paragraph to provide a holistic evaluation.
Suggestion: Add subheadings (e.g., âGross Profit Margin,â âNet Profit Marginâ) within the financial analysis section to improve structure and readability.
Suggestion: Refine final phrasing to maintain academic tone (e.g., avoid colloquial terms like âreally succeedâ).
Weakness: Bibliography entries are inconsistently formatted; establish and apply a uniform citation style (APA, MLA, etc.).
Suggestion: Include DOIs or stable URLs with consistent access dates for all sources to improve verification and presentation quality.
